explicit_text: I'm originally from Uganda, though my journey took me through several countries before I settled in the United States. I was born and raised there, and although I didn't report as a journalist while in Uganda, I was a student at the time. Later, I returned briefly to Uganda in 2010 after fleeing Rwanda before moving on to Kenya. Uganda was part of the regional focus of the news website I helped run, covering several East African countries.
implicit_text: I'm originally from a country in East Africa, though my journey took me through several countries before I settled in the United States. I was born and raised there, and although I didn't report as a journalist while in that country, I was a student at the time. Later, I returned briefly in 2010 after fleeing a neighboring country before moving on to another East African nation. This country was part of the regional focus of the news website I helped run, covering several East African countries.

explicit_text: I spent about ten years working as a journalist in Rwanda, starting when I was 26 years old. It was there that I teamed up with friends to start a regional news website covering Rwanda, Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, Sudan, and the Democratic Republic of the Congo. I left Rwanda on February 2, 2010, fleeing due to circumstances that made it unsafe, and that marked the beginning of my journey through several countries before arriving in the US. My journalism work in Rwanda was intense, focusing on human rights violations and corruption scandals.
implicit_text: I spent about ten years working as a journalist in a central African country, starting when I was 26 years old. It was there that I teamed up with friends to start a regional news website covering that country, plus Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, Sudan, and the Democratic Republic of the Congo. I left that country on February 2, 2010, fleeing due to circumstances that made it unsafe, and that marked the beginning of my journey through several countries before arriving in the US. My journalism work there was intense, focusing on human rights violations and corruption scandals.

explicit_text: After fleeing Rwanda and briefly passing through Uganda, I crossed into Kenya where I stayed from early 2010 until June 5, 2013. It was in Kenya that I began the process to come to the United States. While in Kenya, I continued running the regional news website for about a year even after going into exile. Kenya was a critical stop in my journey, serving as a base for my journalism and preparations to resettle in the US.
implicit_text: After fleeing the central African country and briefly passing through an East African nation, I crossed into another East African country where I stayed from early 2010 until June 5, 2013. It was in this country that I began the process to come to the United States. While there, I continued running the regional news website for about a year even after going into exile. This country was a critical stop in my journey, serving as a base for my journalism and preparations to resettle in the US.

explicit_text: I arrived in the United States on June 6, 2013, after a long and complicated journey through East Africa. Since arriving, I've been pursuing further education in journalism and politics, aiming to refine my skills and eventually launch a media project called Refugee World to give refugees a voice. I've written opinion pieces for the Dayton Daily News, covering topics like racism and the importance of wearing masks during the Coronavirus pandemic. The US has offered me opportunities to grow both academically and professionally in journalism.

I've written opinion pieces for a local newspaper in Ohio, covering topics like racism and the importance of wearing masks during the recent global pandemic. This country has offered me opportunities to grow both academically and professionally in journalism.

explicit_text: The Democratic Republic of the Congo was one of the countries covered by the regional news website I helped run while I was in Rwanda. Alongside Rwanda, Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, and Sudan, DRC stories were part of our broad regional focus. Although I didn't live there, the issues in DRC were important to our journalism work, reflecting the interconnected nature of East and Central African affairs.

Although I never lived in Sudan, its news was relevant to our audience and part of the broader East African context we reported on.

explicit_text: The Coronavirus pandemic has affected my work, especially in terms of launching my project Refugee World, which aims to give refugees a platform to share their stories. Due to the pandemic, I have been delayed in completing registration and legal paperwork for the project in the US. Additionally, I wrote a letter to the editor in the Dayton Daily News advocating for mask-wearing based on scientific evidence to help prevent the virus's spread.

explicit_text: I'm currently studying at Princeton University, where my major is politics and I'm working to get a minor in journalism. I started focusing on journalism relatively late, near the end of my sophomore year, but I've found it very inspiring and aligned with my passion for storytelling and advocacy. Princeton has given me the opportunity to refine my skills and prepare for my future media project focused on refugee voices.

explicit_text: I contribute opinion pieces to the Dayton Daily News, where I've written about important social issues like racism and public health measures during the Coronavirus pandemic. For example, two weeks ago I wrote an opinion piece about equality and how it should start at home, schools, and churches. Just recently, I submitted a letter to the editor advocating for mask-wearing based on scientific advice. Writing for Dayton Daily News allows me to stay connected to journalism and influence public discourse in the US.

explicit_text: I identify as a Christian and was born into a very religious family that is active in the Anglican Church. Christianity has been a significant part of my life since birth, shaping my values and practices. I attend church regularly and follow Christian rituals, which provide me with spiritual support, especially during my journey as a refugee and resettlement in the US.

Christianity has been a significant part of my life since birth, shaping my values and practices. I attend church regularly and follow Christian rituals, which provide me with spiritual support, especially during my journey as a refugee and resettlement in the US.

explicit_text: I was born, baptized, and confirmed in the Anglican Church, which is very strong in many African countries including mine. My family is deeply involved in Anglicanism, and I continue to practice its rituals and attend church services when I can. Anglicanism has been a core part of my religious identity and community life throughout my journey from Uganda to the United States.

explicit_text: The Catholic Church is one of the major traditional religions in Africa, often seen as a competitor to the Anglican Church in many communities. While I was not a member of the Catholic Church myself, I recognize its significant presence alongside Anglicanism, Islam, and the Seventh-day Adventist Church in the region. These religious institutions all play important roles in the social and cultural life of African communities.

explicit_text: Islam is one of the major traditional religions in Africa alongside Christianity and the Seventh-day Adventist Church. Although I am not Muslim, I am aware of Islam's strong presence and influence in many African countries, including those covered by my journalism work. The religious diversity, including Islam, shapes much of the social fabric in the region and affects how communities interact and support refugees.

These religious organizations often assist refugees and displaced persons, especially during the long and difficult resettlement processes.

explicit_text: The International Organization for Migration, or IOM, plays a crucial role in the refugee resettlement process to the United States. They handle the travel arrangements for refugees, including booking air tickets and organizing flights from various parts of the world to US airports. Their support is vital in ensuring refugees can safely and efficiently complete the long journey to start new lives here.
